prompt:
The maintenance vendors upload vehicle service records to our fleet portal as raw JSON, and the hand-rolled if/else checks in the intake worker have drifted into uselessness — half the bad uploads get through and the error strings are different in every branch. We're standardizing on zod for this; package.json already pins the version we run everywhere (plus the committed package-lock.json), and run_verify.sh is what CI executes. Nothing is installed on this box yet — bring the env up from the lockfile with npm ci (runner discipline: never plain `npm install` there), then build schemas.mjs.

Exports:

1. recordSchema — a zod schema for one service record. Every record carries: vehicle {plate, fleet}, odometerKm, date, plus a type field that selects the variant (use a discriminated union). Details: plate is a string that gets TRIMMED and UPPERCASED during parsing and must then match /^[A-Z]{1,3}-\d{3,4}$/ (custom message: "plate must look like ABC-123"); fleet is one of north|south|central; odometerKm is an integer >= 0 but the scanner app sends it as a string half the time, so coerce numeric strings; date is a "YYYY-MM-DD" string that must be a real calendar day — reject things like 2026-02-30 (custom message: "date must be a real YYYY-MM-DD day"). Variants: type "service" adds intervalKm (positive integer, and it must be a multiple of 5000 — message "intervalKm must be a multiple of 5000") and checklist (array of non-empty strings, at least one entry). Type "repair" adds failureCode (enum ENG|BRK|ELE|SUS), workshop (string that can't be blank — message "workshop is required"), costCents (integer >= 0), and parts, a possibly-empty array of {sku, qty} where sku must match /^P-\d{5}$/ (message "sku must look like P-12345") and qty is an integer >= 1. Type "inspection" adds passed (boolean) and notes (string, optional, defaults to ""), with one cross-field rule: a failed inspection (passed false) must carry non-empty notes — report that at path notes with message "failed inspection needs notes". Vendors typo field names constantly and we'd rather bounce than guess: every object level is STRICT, unknown keys are an error. A type we don't recognize must come back as a single issue at path type.

2. parseRecord(data) — never throws; safeParse under the hood. Returns {ok: true, record} with the parsed/transformed record, or {ok: false, issues} where issues is [{path, code, message}] — path is the zod path joined with dots (array indices as plain numbers, so parts.0.qty; root-level issues get ""), code is zod's issue code, message is the message. Sort issues by path (tie-break on code) so the vendor report is stable.

3. parseBatch(list) — for the nightly NDJSON batch, already decoded to an array. Returns {accepted, rejected}: accepted is the parsed records in input order, rejected is [{index, issues}] with the original array index. Anything that isn't an array should throw a TypeError with message "batch must be an array".

test_schemas.mjs is the contract — it pins the issue shapes exactly (codes, dotted paths, the custom messages above). Done when `bash run_verify.sh` passes; it runs the suite out of the project's node_modules.
